project.name: Tack Timetable
project.description: AI-powered school timetable generator using Next.js App Router + React + TypeScript, with a Python CP-SAT solver backend and Electron desktop app wrapper.

nodes.name: ApiKeyScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: React component that renders the API key entry screen, validates the submitted key against the backend, and persists it to the store upon successful activation.

nodes.name: ApiKeyScreen
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component that provides an input form for entering and activating an API key, with inline validation feedback and success state handling.

nodes.name: QuotaDisplay.tsx
nodes.summary: React component that polls for and displays the current API quota usage, refreshing at regular intervals and showing token counts in a human-readable format.

nodes.name: QuotaDisplay
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component that fetches the model policy quota on mount and on an interval, then renders used and remaining token counts.

nodes.name: TimetableApp.tsx
nodes.summary: The primary multi-page React application component for the timetable generator, managing all wizard steps from schedule setup and data entry (teachers, subjects, classes, assignments, constraints) through AI-driven generation and the resulting timetable spreadsheet view.
nodes.languageNotes: Monolithic 2818-line component file combining multiple sub-components (SelectField, DayTile, SessionTile, PeriodControl, InfoField) and the main App component in a single file — characteristic of a heavily iterative feature build.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Main application shell component that orchestrates all timetable wizard pages, manages all feature state (teachers, subjects, classes, assignments, constraints, AI result), and drives the timetable generation flow via the AI client.

nodes.name: PeriodControl
nodes.summary: Controlled numeric input component for specifying period counts per session, with clamping, step buttons, and keyboard navigation.

nodes.name: handleGenerate
nodes.summary: Async handler that validates prerequisites, invokes the AI timetable generation client with a streaming progress callback, and maps progress events to UI agent-status state.

nodes.name: api-key-store.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing the AI API key state, quota/model-policy data, and persistence to localStorage, along with formatting helpers for token counts.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zustand with a custom hydration function to rehydrate the API key from localStorage on app startup, a common SSR-safe pattern for Next.js.

nodes.name: useApiKeyStore
nodes.summary: Zustand store hook exported for use across components, providing API key state, setter, and model policy fetch logic.

nodes.name: hydrateApiKeyFromStorage
nodes.summary: Reads the stored API key from localStorage and pushes it into the Zustand store, also triggering a model-policy fetch if the key is present.

nodes.name: formatTokenCount
nodes.summary: Formats a raw integer token count into a human-readable string with M/K suffix, e.g. 1500000 → '1.5M'.

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: HTTP client for the timetable generation API, supporting both non-streaming and streaming (SSE) response modes and forwarding progress events to a callback.

nodes.name: generateTimetableWithAI
nodes.summary: Sends a timetable generation request to the backend API; handles both streaming SSE mode and non-streaming fallback, parsing progress events and returning the final result.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definitions for the AI timetable generation domain, including request payloads, solver results, violation records, progress events, and solver statistics.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure TypeScript interface and type alias file with no runtime code; acts as the shared contract between the frontend client and the API route.

nodes.name: constants.ts
nodes.summary: Application-wide constants for the timetable feature: school day definitions, session configurations, default period counts, class and subject presets, teacher color palette, and shared Tailwind CSS class strings.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Thin utility module exporting pure helper functions for normalizing subject names, generating assignment and cell key strings, sorting lists alphabetically, and computing slot keys.

nodes.name: normalizeSubjectName
nodes.summary: Normalizes a subject name string by trimming and applying a canonical alias map lookup.

nodes.name: makeAssignmentKey
nodes.summary: Generates a unique string key for a teacher-subject-class-periods assignment tuple.

nodes.name: sortAlphabetically
nodes.summary: Returns a locale-sorted copy of a string array.

nodes.name: getCellKey
nodes.summary: Builds a composite string key from day ID, session ID, and period number for identifying a timetable cell.

nodes.name: getAssignmentSlotKey
nodes.summary: Builds a composite string key from teacher, class name, and slot ID for identifying a schedule assignment slot.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Python package entry point for the timetable_solver module, re-exporting the core solver interface and base template symbols for external consumers.

nodes.name: base_solver_template.py
nodes.summary: Provides the reusable CP-SAT constraint model infrastructure — building the base binary assignment model, managing hard/soft constraint literals, running the solver, and assembling structured result objects.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Google OR-Tools CP-SAT solver with assumption-based IIS (Irreducible Infeasible Subsystem) detection for diagnosing unsatisfiable hard constraints.

nodes.name: runner.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that reads a JSON solve request from stdin, dynamically loads a user-supplied solver artifact via importlib, invokes its entrypoint function, and writes the result or error as JSON to stdout.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location to load solver modules at runtime from an arbitrary file path, enabling hot-swappable solver implementations.

nodes.name: template_solver.py
nodes.summary: The primary timetable constraint solver implementing the full scheduling logic — parsing hard/soft constraints, translating domain rules (teacher conflicts, consecutive limits, free-day preferences, block scheduling) into CP-SAT model callbacks, and delegating to solve_base_model.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines an inner extra_setup callback passed to solve_base_model, giving the base template a clean extension point for domain-specific constraint types without coupling the base to template business logic.

nodes.name: build_empty_result
nodes.summary: Constructs a standardised solver result dictionary with status, message, diagnostics, assignment cells, IIS constraint IDs, and solver statistics — used to return both success and failure outcomes.

nodes.name: build_solver_stats
nodes.summary: Extracts wall-time, objective value, best bound, conflicts, and branch count from a completed CP-SAT solver instance into a plain stats dictionary.

nodes.name: normalize_problem
nodes.summary: Normalises the raw problem dict by extracting assignments, meta, and solver_config fields with safe defaults.

nodes.name: create_base_model
nodes.summary: Builds the CP-SAT base model with binary decision variables for each (assignment, slot) pair, enforcing that every assignment is placed in exactly one slot and no slot is over-allocated.

nodes.name: solve_base_model
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full solve pipeline: validates inputs, creates the base model, calls the extra_setup callback for domain constraints, runs the CP-SAT solver with a time limit, interprets OPTIMAL/FEASIBLE/INFEASIBLE results, performs IIS analysis on failure, and returns a structured result object.

nodes.name: _load_solver_entrypoint
nodes.summary: Dynamically loads a Python module from a file path using importlib and returns the named entrypoint callable, raising descriptive errors for missing files, invalid specs, or missing attributes.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Reads a JSON request from stdin, resolves and invokes the appropriate solver entrypoint, and writes either the solver result or a structured error payload as JSON to stdout, then exits.

nodes.name: _normalize_parsed
nodes.summary: Normalises parsed constraint items from the structured format into a canonical list of dicts with consistent fields for assignment IDs, slot IDs, and labels.

nodes.name: _legacy_parse
nodes.summary: Parses legacy free-text constraint descriptions using regex patterns to extract day names, period numbers, and ranges for hard and soft constraints expressed in natural language.

nodes.name: _slots
nodes.summary: Resolves a list of slot IDs from meta lookup tables given a key and a list of values, deduplicating results.

nodes.name: _slot_ids_for_day_period
nodes.summary: Returns deduplicated slot IDs matching the given day IDs and period numbers from the meta slot lookup.

nodes.name: _slot_ids_for_session_day
nodes.summary: Returns deduplicated slot IDs matching the given session IDs and day IDs from the meta slot lookup.

nodes.name: _labels_to_asgs
nodes.summary: Translates a list of label strings to assignment IDs using a label map, supporting wildcard resolution for all assignments.

nodes.name: _all_slots
nodes.summary: Returns the set of all slot IDs across all days from the meta slotsByDayId mapping.

nodes.name: _sorted_day_slots
nodes.summary: Returns slots for a given day sorted by their slot map index, enabling ordered consecutive-constraint processing.

nodes.name: _force_zero
nodes.summary: Adds conditional CP-SAT constraints that force all (assignment, slot) variables in the given sets to zero when a literal is true, implementing hard exclusion constraints.

nodes.name: solve_timetable
nodes.summary: Top-level solver entrypoint that parses hard/soft constraints, builds an extra_setup closure encoding all domain-specific scheduling rules (teacher conflicts, consecutive limits, block scheduling, free-day preferences, synchronisation), and delegates to solve_base_model.

nodes.name: benchmark-datasets.mjs
nodes.summary: Developer benchmark script that parses a plain-text dataset file, runs the Python timetable solver with both adaptive and legacy solver configurations, and prints a JSON performance comparison report.

nodes.name: sandbox.ts
nodes.summary: Core Node.js-to-Python bridge that spawns the Python timetable solver subprocess, resolves paths to the runner and Python binary across packaged and development environments, enforces a configurable timeout, and returns typed JSON results.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Node.js child_process.spawn with piped stdio to communicate with the Python solver; supports both packaged (TIMETABLE_PYTHON_RUNNER_DIR env var) and local venv layouts.

nodes.name: solver-worker.ts
nodes.summary: Thin adapter that wraps runSolverDirect from sandbox.ts behind a SolverWorkerClient interface, providing a uniform run/close API for callers that expect a worker-style client.

nodes.name: timetable-prompt.ts
nodes.summary: Transforms raw UI timetable inputs (days, sessions, period counts, assignments, constraints) into a normalized InputPayload structure, and provides an adaptive solver configuration estimator based on problem complexity.

nodes.name: runSolverDirect
nodes.summary: Spawns the Python solver subprocess with the correct binary and PYTHONPATH, writes the serialized problem via stdin, enforces a dynamic timeout, and resolves with parsed JSON output or a structured error.

nodes.name: resolvePythonBin
nodes.summary: Determines the Python executable path by checking the TIMETABLE_PYTHON_BIN env var, then probing for a local venv on both Unix and Windows, falling back to system python3/python.

nodes.name: createSolverWorkerClient
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a SolverWorkerClient object delegating run calls directly to runSolverDirect, with a no-op close method.

nodes.name: buildInputPayload
nodes.summary: Converts raw day/session/assignment/constraint inputs into a fully normalized InputPayload: enumerates valid slots (skipping deleted periods), assigns deterministic IDs to teachers, subjects, and classes, and separates hard and soft constraints.

nodes.name: estimateSolverConfig
nodes.summary: Computes adaptive solver configuration (maxTimeSeconds, numWorkers) based on a complexity metric derived from the product of slot count and assignment count.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on every push and pull request: installs JS and Python dependencies, executes ESLint, Next.js build, pytest unit tests, and conditional dataset API integration tests against a live dev server.

nodes.name: release-windows.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes the Windows Electron desktop release on version tags or manual dispatch: compiles the Python solver with PyInstaller, builds the Next.js app, packages the Electron app, and publishes artifacts as a GitHub release.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a multi-stage build strategy: PyInstaller bundles the Python solver as a standalone executable, then Electron Forge/packager wraps the Next.js app for Windows distribution.

nodes.name: Build Python runner
nodes.summary: PyInstaller step that compiles python/timetable_solver/runner.py into a standalone Windows executable placed in python-dist/runner.

nodes.name: Build Electron artifacts
nodes.summary: Runs npm run package:win to bundle the Electron desktop application for Windows, producing distributable artifacts in the release/ directory.

nodes.name: Publish GitHub release assets
nodes.summary: Conditionally publishes all files in release/ as GitHub release assets using softprops/action-gh-release, only when the triggering ref is a version tag.

nodes.name: .env
nodes.summary: Minimal environment variable file defining a single DATABASE_URL placeholder for local development runtime configuration.

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration registering the GitNexus code intelligence CLI and a local forge extension HTTP endpoint for AI agent tooling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses both stdio (local binary) and HTTP URL transports for MCP servers — typical for mixed local/remote agent toolchains.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: AI agent operating instructions covering workspace rules, workflow guidelines, important file references, environment/secrets policy, verification checklist, deploy notes, and GitNexus code-intelligence usage protocols.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for Tack Timetable describing the two UI variants (primary Next.js App Router and legacy Vite app), the end-to-end timetable generation flow with key file references, and local verification instructions.

nodes.name: Walkthrough.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive architectural design document and implementation checklist covering the AI-powered agentic timetable generation system, including Coder/Checker agent loops, deterministic validator layer, data contracts, SSE events, UI plan, Excel export, guardrails, and phased rollout plan.

nodes.name: benchmark_latest.json
nodes.summary: Benchmark results data comparing legacy and adaptive solver performance across multiple datasets, recording elapsed time, solver status, wall-time seconds, and branch/conflict counts per dataset.

nodes.name: benchmark_latest_tuned.json
nodes.summary: Tuned variant of benchmark results comparing legacy and adaptive solver performance, used to evaluate parameter-tuned solver configurations against the baseline across the same dataset suite.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui component library configuration specifying the new-york style, RSC and TSX support, Tailwind CSS variables and globals path, path aliases for components/utils/ui/lib/hooks, and the Lucide icon library.

nodes.name: datasets.txt
nodes.summary: Large text file containing raw timetable dataset definitions used as test inputs for the solver and benchmark suite, covering multiple Vietnamese school scheduling scenarios.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root NPM package manifest for the Tack Timetable project (v3.0.10) defining Next.js build/dev/lint scripts, Electron desktop packaging scripts, Python solver runner script, and all frontend dependencies including Radix UI, shadcn, Prisma, and AI SDK packages.
nodes.languageNotes: Dual-target project: runs as both a Next.js web app and an Electron desktop app — evidenced by separate dist:win/dist:linux scripts and electron-builder configuration.

nodes.name: scripts_test_generate_timetable.sh
nodes.summary: Shell smoke-test script that validates the generate-timetable API endpoint by running pytest against test_datasets.py, requiring a LOWPRIZO_API_KEY environment variable and accepting an optional base URL argument.

nodes.name: skills-lock.json
nodes.summary: Claude Code skills lock file pinning the installed agent-browser skill to a specific GitHub source and content hash for reproducible AI agent tool installations.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the Next.js app enabling strict mode with ES2017 target, bundler module resolution, React JSX transform, incremental compilation, and a `@/*` path alias mapping to `src/`.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `moduleResolution: bundler` (Next.js 13+ recommended setting) and the next TypeScript plugin for enhanced IDE support.

nodes.name: worklog.md
nodes.summary: Agent work log recording two completed tasks: initial porting of the Vite+React timetable app to Next.js, and centering the timetable content layout to match a reference design.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Documentation skill file for the agent-browser CLI tool, describing how to invoke browser automation, specialized sub-skills, and an observability dashboard.

nodes.name: autopilot-log.json
nodes.summary: Autopilot run log persisted by the claude-flow automation system, recording iteration events.

nodes.name: autopilot-state.json
nodes.summary: Persisted runtime state for the claude-flow autopilot session, including session ID, iteration counters, timeout settings, and task sources.

nodes.name: mcp.json
nodes.summary: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration for the Roo environment, listing server endpoints accessible to the AI agent.

nodes.name: memory.db
nodes.summary: SQLite binary database file used by the RuFlo swarm memory system for agent memory storage.

nodes.name: memory.db-shm
nodes.summary: SQLite shared-memory file accompanying the swarm memory database for WAL mode coordination.

nodes.name: memory.db-wal
nodes.summary: SQLite write-ahead log for the swarm memory database, containing pending transactions not yet checkpointed to the main db file.

nodes.name: schema.sql
nodes.summary: RuFlo V3 SQLite schema defining tables for agent memory entries, pattern learning, trajectory tracking, migration state, sessions, and vector indexes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses WAL journal mode, vector embeddings stored as JSON arrays, and includes temporal decay fields (expires_at, last_accessed_at) for hot/cold memory detection.

nodes.name: memory_entries
nodes.summary: Core memory storage table for semantic, episodic, procedural, working, and pattern memory with vector embedding support and temporal decay.

nodes.name: patterns
nodes.summary: Pattern learning table tracking success/failure counts, confidence scores, and hierarchical pattern relationships for agent behavioral adaptation.

nodes.name: trajectories
nodes.summary: Trajectory recording table for agent session paths, storing verdict, context, and total reward for reinforcement-style learning.

nodes.name: sessions
nodes.summary: Agent session tracking table recording branch, patterns learned, and expiry for session-scoped memory management.

nodes.name: vector_indexes
nodes.summary: Metadata registry for HNSW vector index configurations including metric type, construction and search ef parameters, and quantization settings.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern file for the understand-anything code analysis tool, listing directories and file patterns to exclude from knowledge graph indexing.

nodes.name: Caddyfile
nodes.summary: Caddy reverse proxy configuration routing port 81 to the Next.js app on port 3000, with a special query-parameter-based port transform for dynamic upstream selection.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Caddy's named matcher syntax to selectively proxy based on an XTransformPort query parameter, enabling dynamic port forwarding.

nodes.name: dev.db
nodes.summary: Empty SQLite development database file, likely the Prisma-managed local database for the Next.js app.

nodes.name: main.mjs
nodes.summary: Electron main process entry point that bootstraps the Tack Timetable desktop app by spawning the bundled Next.js server and Python solver, then loading the app in a BrowserWindow.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles both dev mode (proxies to localhost:3000) and packaged mode (spawns standalone Next.js server from asar.unpacked resources), with platform-specific Python binary resolution.

nodes.name: startServer
nodes.summary: Spawns the bundled Next.js standalone server process with the correct environment variables and waits for it to become ready.

nodes.name: createWindow
nodes.summary: Creates the Electron BrowserWindow, shows a loading screen, then navigates to the running Next.js app URL or an error page on failure.

nodes.name: waitForServer
nodes.summary: Polls an HTTP endpoint up to 240 times (60 seconds) until the server responds with a non-5xx status.

nodes.name: getPythonBin
nodes.summary: Resolves the Python executable path by checking environment variable override, bundled executables, and dev venv before falling back to system python.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: ESLint flat configuration for the Next.js TypeScript project, enabling recommended and Next.js core-web-vitals rules.

nodes.name: frontend.tsx
nodes.summary: Example React component demonstrating a Socket.IO-based real-time chat room with username setup, message sending, and online user tracking.

nodes.name: SocketDemo
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component implementing a Socket.IO chat room UI with connection lifecycle management, message display, and user roster.

nodes.name: server.ts
nodes.summary: Example Socket.IO server implementation showing connection handling, room management, and message broadcasting patterns.

nodes.name: .gitkeep
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file keeping the mini-services directory tracked in git.

nodes.name: next-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Next.js TypeScript environment declaration file that references Next.js type definitions.

nodes.name: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js configuration enabling standalone output mode for Electron packaging, disabling image optimization, excluding release/dist/venv from file tracing, and suppressing TypeScript build errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Standalone output mode is critical for the Electron desktop build — it produces a self-contained server bundle that can be shipped inside an asar package.

nodes.name: postcss.config.mjs
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling the Tailwind CSS plugin for the Next.js project.

nodes.name: schema.prisma
nodes.summary: Prisma schema defining User, Post, and ApiKey models backed by a SQLite database, providing the ORM layer for the Next.js application.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SQLite provider with DATABASE_URL from environment; ApiKey model suggests API authentication support.

nodes.name: robots.txt
nodes.summary: Robots exclusion file for the web app, controlling crawler access to public pages.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest configuration file for the Python solver package, adding the python directory to sys.path for test discovery.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency list for the timetable solver package.

nodes.name: ruvector.db
nodes.summary: SQLite database file used by the ruvector embedding/vector storage system.

nodes.name: benchmark_datasets.py
nodes.summary: Benchmarking script that parses timetable datasets from a text file, constructs solver problems, and runs the Python solver to measure performance with and without adaptive configuration.

nodes.name: parse_datasets
nodes.summary: Parses a structured text file containing multiple timetable dataset blocks, extracting teachers, subjects, classes, days, time slots, and assignment constraints.

nodes.name: build_problem
nodes.summary: Transforms a parsed dataset dictionary into a structured solver problem object with normalized IDs for teachers, subjects, classes, and time slots.

nodes.name: run_solver
nodes.summary: Executes the Python timetable solver as a subprocess, measuring wall-clock time and returning the solution result with status.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that iterates all datasets, runs each through the solver with default and adaptive configs, collects results, and prints a JSON summary.

nodes.name: post-build.js
nodes.summary: Cross-platform post-build script that copies static assets, public files, and next-server into the .next/standalone directory for Docker deployment, and removes accidentally traced Python workspaces.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler for managing the user's API key — supports GET (retrieve masked key), POST (save a new key), and DELETE (clear stored key) via Prisma database access.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Returns the stored API key in masked form (first 4 and last 4 chars visible) or null if none exists.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Saves a new API key by deleting all existing keys and inserting the new trimmed value, with validation for empty input.

nodes.name: DELETE
nodes.summary: Deletes all stored API key records from the database.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js POST route handler that receives timetable generation requests and streams progress events via Server-Sent Events (SSE) or returns a JSON result, delegating heavy lifting to the agentic loop service.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ReadableStream with a TextEncoder to implement SSE without a third-party library.

nodes.name: createSSEStream
nodes.summary: Creates a ReadableStream-based SSE channel with send and close helpers that serialize events as JSON-encoded data lines.

nodes.name: POST
nodes.summary: Main request handler that parses the timetable generation input, resolves API key and model, then runs the agentic loop either synchronously (JSON response) or as an SSE stream based on Accept header.

nodes.name: service.ts
nodes.summary: Core agentic timetable generation service implementing a multi-round loop: LLM-driven code generation, sandboxed solver execution, deterministic validation, and iterative checker feedback until a valid schedule is produced or attempts are exhausted.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a code-generate → execute → validate → check → retry loop with attempt tracking, token estimation, and artifact persistence for Python solver scripts.

nodes.name: runAgenticLoop
nodes.summary: Top-level orchestrator for the multi-round timetable generation loop — normalizes input, iterates coder attempts and checker validation, emits progress events, and returns a finalized result with all attempt metadata.

nodes.name: runCoderAttempt
nodes.summary: Executes a single LLM coder attempt: calls the LLM with system+user prompts to generate a solver script, persists the artifact, and emits a progress event.

nodes.name: executeSolver
nodes.summary: Runs a generated solver script in the sandbox and emits progress and result events, handling execution errors and empty timetable guard.

nodes.name: shouldContinueLoop
nodes.summary: Determines whether the agentic loop should continue based on time budget, attempt count, and whether a valid solution has already been found.

nodes.name: finalizeResult
nodes.summary: Builds the final timetable generation result object from the best solver output, including constraint violations, IIS constraints, and artifact summary.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js GET route that proxies model policy data from the upstream Lowprizo API, extracting and forwarding the API key from request headers with Bearer token and custom header support.

nodes.name: GET
nodes.summary: Fetches model policy from upstream API using the caller's API key, returning quota and model availability data.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal health-check API route that returns a 200 OK JSON response for the root API path.

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS file that applies Tailwind base/components/utilities layers and defines CSS custom property design tokens for the light and dark color themes used across the application.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS custom properties (variables) with @layer for scoped Tailwind integration and dual light/dark theme token sets.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root Next.js App Router layout that sets application metadata, applies the Geist font, and wraps all pages with the global CSS baseline.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Root layout component that wraps the HTML body with Geist font classes and renders child pages.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Main page component for the timetable app that renders the full application UI including settings panel, API key management, model policy quota display, and the timetable generator interface.

nodes.name: Home
nodes.summary: Top-level page component that manages settings panel visibility, API key state, model policy data, and renders the complete timetable generator UI with settings overlay and quota display.

nodes.name: TimetableApp.jsx
nodes.summary: Thin re-export shim that forwards the default export from the main TimetableApp feature component, bridging the components directory to the features directory.

nodes.name: accordion.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Accordion component providing collapsible content sections built on Radix UI Accordion primitives with Tailwind-styled trigger and content animations.

nodes.name: AccordionTrigger
nodes.summary: Styled accordion trigger button with chevron icon that rotates on open state.

nodes.name: AccordionContent
nodes.summary: Styled accordion content area with animated expand/collapse transitions.

nodes.name: alert-dialog.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui AlertDialog component set built on Radix UI AlertDialog primitives, exporting overlay, content, header, footer, title, description, action, and cancel sub-components.

nodes.name: AlertDialogContent
nodes.summary: Renders the dialog content pane inside a portal with overlay, applying Tailwind animation and layout classes.

nodes.name: alert.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Alert component with default and destructive variants, plus AlertTitle and AlertDescription sub-components for structured notification messages.

nodes.name: aspect-ratio.tsx
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper re-exporting the Radix UI AspectRatio primitive as a named export for consistent import paths.

nodes.name: avatar.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Avatar component built on Radix UI primitives, exporting Avatar, AvatarImage, and AvatarFallback with rounded Tailwind styling.

nodes.name: badge.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Badge component with cva-based variant system (default, secondary, destructive, outline) supporting asChild composition.

nodes.name: breadcrumb.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Breadcrumb navigation component set with seven exported sub-components including list, item, link, page, separator, and ellipsis.

nodes.name: button.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Button component with cva-based variant and size system (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) plus asChild support via Radix Slot.

nodes.name: Button
nodes.summary: Core button component applying cva variant classes and delegating rendering to Slot or native button based on asChild prop.

nodes.name: calendar.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Calendar component built on react-day-picker v9, exporting Calendar and CalendarDayButton with full class name customization and navigation button variant support.
nodes.languageNotes: Built on react-day-picker v9 API which differs significantly from v8; uses getDefaultClassNames() for merge-safe class composition.

nodes.name: Calendar
nodes.summary: Full calendar component with extensive class name overrides for all react-day-picker sub-elements, navigation button variants, and locale-aware month formatting.

nodes.name: card.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Card component set exporting Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardAction, CardContent, and CardFooter as styled container building blocks.

nodes.name: carousel.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Carousel component built on Embla Carousel, providing Carousel, CarouselContent, CarouselItem, CarouselPrevious, and CarouselNext with horizontal/vertical orientation support and keyboard navigation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a React Context pattern (CarouselContext) and a custom useCarousel hook to share Embla API and scroll state across sub-components.

nodes.name: Carousel
nodes.summary: Root carousel component that initializes Embla, manages scroll state and callbacks, handles keyboard navigation, and provides context to child components.

nodes.name: chart.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui Chart component infrastructure built on Recharts, providing ChartContainer with dynamic CSS variable injection, ChartStyle for theme-aware color tokens, ChartTooltipContent, and ChartLegendContent.
nodes.languageNotes: Injects per-chart CSS custom properties (--color-*) via a style tag keyed by chart ID, enabling Recharts color references without inline styles.

nodes.name: ChartContainer
nodes.summary: Wrapper that provides chart configuration via context, generates a unique chart ID, and renders ChartStyle alongside the ResponsiveContainer.

nodes.name: ChartTooltipContent
nodes.summary: Recharts tooltip content renderer with configurable indicator styles (dot, line, dashed), label formatting, color resolution from chart config, and nested item layout.

nodes.name: ChartLegendContent

nodes.name: toast.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/ui Toast component collection built on Radix UI Toast primitives, exporting ToastProvider, ToastViewport, Toast, ToastTitle, ToastDescription, ToastClose, ToastAction, and associated TypeScript types.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses cva (class-variance-authority) for variant-based styling on the Toast root element.

nodes.name: toaster.tsx
nodes.summary: Toaster provider component that reads from the useToast hook and renders all active toasts inside ToastProvider and ToastViewport.

nodes.name: Toaster
nodes.summary: Renders the toast portal by consuming the useToast hook and mapping active toasts to Toast, ToastTitle, and ToastDescription components.

nodes.name: toggle-group.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/ui ToggleGroup and ToggleGroupItem components built on Radix UI Toggle Group, sharing variant and size context via React context for consistent styling across group items.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a custom React context to propagate variant/size props from ToggleGroup down to each ToggleGroupItem without prop drilling.

nodes.name: ToggleGroup
nodes.summary: Root toggle group component that provides a context with variant and size values to its children via a custom context.

nodes.name: ToggleGroupItem
nodes.summary: Individual toggle item that reads variant/size from the ToggleGroup context and applies toggleVariants for consistent Tailwind styling.

nodes.name: toggle.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/ui Toggle component built on Radix UI Toggle primitive, exporting the Toggle component and its toggleVariants cva configuration for use by ToggleGroup.

nodes.name: tooltip.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn/ui Tooltip component set wrapping Radix UI Tooltip primitives, providing TooltipProvider, Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, and TooltipContent with animated slide-in styling.

nodes.name: TooltipContent
nodes.summary: Tooltip content portal with animated fade/slide entrance, positioned relative to the trigger with a configurable sideOffset.

nodes.name: use-mobile.ts
nodes.summary: React hook that detects whether the current viewport is below the mobile breakpoint (768px) using a matchMedia listener with cleanup.

nodes.name: useIsMobile
nodes.summary: Returns a boolean indicating if the viewport is in mobile range, subscribing to matchMedia change events and cleaning up on unmount.

nodes.name: use-toast.ts
nodes.summary: Headless toast state management module implementing a mini pub-sub store with a reducer, timeout-based auto-dismiss queue, and a useToast hook for consuming toast state in React components.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a global singleton state pattern outside React (listeners array, memoryState) rather than Context, allowing toast() to be called from non-component code.

nodes.name: reducer
nodes.summary: Pure reducer handling ADD_TOAST, UPDATE_TOAST, DISMISS_TOAST, and REMOVE_TOAST actions with a configurable TOAST_LIMIT for maximum concurrent toasts.

nodes.name: toast
nodes.summary: Imperative toast creation function that generates a unique ID, dispatches ADD_TOAST, and returns update/dismiss/id handles for programmatic control.

nodes.name: useToast
nodes.summary: React hook that subscribes to the global toast store, returning the current toasts array together with the toast and dismiss functions.

nodes.name: checker.ts
nodes.summary: Provides prompt builder functions for the Checker Agent that evaluates solver results without writing code, returning verdict (pass or retry) based on normalized input and deterministic reports.

nodes.name: coder.ts
nodes.summary: Provides prompt builder functions for the Coder Agent that generates OR-Tools CP-SAT Python solver code following a strict contract (solve_timetable), enforcing base/hard/soft constraint hierarchy.

nodes.name: constraint-parser.ts
nodes.summary: Deterministic Vietnamese-language constraint parser that maps freeform text rules (teacher availability, class limits, consecutive periods, etc.) to structured ParsedConstraint objects using regex and label matching.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports both diacritic (Vietnamese Unicode NFC) and diacritic-stripped (ASCII) variants in all regex patterns to handle inconsistent user input.

nodes.name: parseConstraint
nodes.summary: Main exported entry point that normalizes constraint text and dispatches to typed sub-parsers (maxConsecutive, minOffDays, blockConsecutive, not_day, allowed_day, not_period, allowed_period, etc.), returning a ParsedConstraint.

nodes.name: db.ts
nodes.summary: Initializes and exports the Vercel Postgres database client singleton used for persistent storage across API routes.

nodes.name: deterministic-checker.ts
nodes.summary: Rule-based deterministic constraint verification engine that checks a timetable solution against parsed hard constraints (teacher blocking, allowlists, consecutive limits, min off-days, daily subject requirements) and returns structured violation reports.
nodes.languageNotes: Operates entirely without LLM involvement — all checks are pure TypeScript predicate logic over timetable cell arrays.

nodes.name: checkParsed
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the appropriate constraint-specific check function based on the parsed constraint kind, accumulating Violation objects for each failed rule.

nodes.name: runDeterministicChecks
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that iterates all hard constraints, calls checkParsed for each parseable one, and returns a DeterministicReport with violations and unchecked constraint IDs.

nodes.name: generated-solver-artifacts.ts
nodes.summary: File-system utilities for managing AI-generated Python solver code artifacts — locating the Python source directory, writing solver files per request ID, reading the base solver template, and cleaning up after execution.

nodes.name: persistGeneratedSolverArtifact
nodes.summary: Writes the AI-generated solver Python code to disk for a given request ID, computing a SHA-256 hash of the code and returning path and hash metadata.

nodes.name: cleanupSolverArtifact
nodes.summary: Removes the generated solver artifact directory for a request ID to prevent disk accumulation after solver execution.

nodes.name: timetable-config.ts
nodes.summary: Centralized numeric configuration constants for the timetable generation pipeline — iteration limits, prompt truncation thresholds, and LLM retry delays.

nodes.name: timetable-problem.ts
nodes.summary: Transforms a raw timetable generation request and parsed payload into a fully normalized NormalizedSolverProblem object, including constraint parsing, slot/assignment mapping, and solver configuration estimation.

nodes.name: buildNormalizedSolverProblem
nodes.summary: Main entry point that assembles the complete NormalizedSolverProblem from request, payload, and requestId — calling summarizePayload, buildNormalizedConstraints, estimateSolverConfig, and mapping slots and assignments.

nodes.name: buildNormalizedConstraints
nodes.summary: Maps a list of raw constraint strings through parseConstraint with context and attaches user-provided confirmations to produce NormalizedConstraint objects.

nodes.name: timetable-validator.ts
nodes.summary: Validates a timetable solver result against base integrity rules (slot coverage, teacher/class conflicts) and hard/soft constraint checks, producing a structured ValidationResult with pass/fail flags and check details.

nodes.name: validateTimetableResult
nodes.summary: Orchestrates base, hard, and soft validation checks on a solver result, returning a ValidationResult object with boolean pass flags, failed check arrays, and unparsed constraint IDs.

nodes.name: buildBaseChecks
nodes.summary: Builds base integrity checks verifying that all required slots are filled, all assignments are scheduled, and no teacher or class is double-booked in the same slot.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Single-function utility module exporting cn(), a Tailwind class merge helper combining clsx and tailwind-merge for conflict-free className composition.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Merges class name inputs using clsx for conditional logic and tailwind-merge to deduplicate conflicting Tailwind utilities.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.ts
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration defining content paths, dark mode strategy (class), and an extended theme with custom CSS variable-based colors, border radius tokens, and keyframe animations for the accordion component.
nodes.languageNotes: All color and radius tokens use CSS custom properties (var(--...)) so they integrate with Shadcn/ui's design token system.

nodes.name: test_datasets.py
nodes.summary: Pytest integration test suite that parses structured dataset files, constructs timetable API payloads, calls the local generate-timetable endpoint, and asserts solver output completeness and entry validity.

nodes.name: parse_datasets
nodes.summary: Reads and parses a structured plain-text dataset file, splitting it into individual test dataset blocks and extracting days, sessions, max periods, assignments, and constraint strings.

nodes.name: test_dataset_api
nodes.summary: Parametrized pytest test that calls the timetable generation API with a dataset-derived payload and asserts the result has a valid schedule with filled cells and properly assigned entries.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: Next.js app shell, timetable feature UI components (multi-step wizard, API key screen, quota display), and the full shadcn/ui component library used to build the timetable generator interface.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Next.js App Router route handlers exposing the timetable generation SSE endpoint, API key management CRUD, model policy proxy, and health-check routes.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core timetable business logic including the AI HTTP client, Zustand API key store, solver bridge, constraint parser, deterministic validator, problem normalizer, prompt builders, agent prompt templates, React hooks, and shared utilities.

layers.name: Python Solver
layers.description: Python CP-SAT constraint-programming backend implementing the school timetable scheduling algorithm, including the base model infrastructure, full constraint solver, CLI runner, package entry point, and test configuration.

layers.name: Desktop App
layers.description: Electron main process that bootstraps the Tack Timetable desktop application by spawning the bundled Next.js server and Python solver subprocess inside a BrowserWindow.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Prisma ORM schema, the local SQLite development database, the RuFlo swarm agent memory database and its SQL schema with vector/session/pattern tables, and benchmark result data files.

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows for CI testing and Windows Electron release packaging, build scripts for post-build asset copying and solver benchmarking, the Caddy reverse proxy config, and the shell smoke-test script.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project-wide build and tooling configuration including Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, ESLint, package manifest, shadcn/ui component config, environment variables, MCP server configs, and auto-generated type declarations.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Integration and dataset-driven tests that validate the generate-timetable API endpoint against real solver outputs, including the pytest suite and example websocket code.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation covering the README overview, architectural design walkthrough, AI agent operating instructions, work log, solver dataset inputs, robots exclusion rules, and the agent-browser skill reference.

layers.name: Developer Tooling
layers.description: Local AI agent tooling infrastructure including the understand-anything ignore config, RuFlo swarm/autopilot runtime state, and placeholder directories for mini-services.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md is the best entry point for understanding Tack Timetable: an AI-powered school schedule generator that combines a Next.js web UI, a Next.js API layer, and a Python CP-SAT constraint solver. It documents both UI variants (the active App Router app and the legacy Vite playground), maps out the full end-to-end generation flow with exact file references, and provides local verification instructions. Reading it first gives you the vocabulary and mental map you will need for every step that follows.

tour.title: App Shell & Entry Point
tour.description: src/app/page.tsx is the Next.js App Router page that users actually land on. It renders the complete application UI — API key management, quota display, and the timetable generator wizard — inside the root layout defined by layout.tsx. Together, layout.tsx and page.tsx form the outermost shell that wires the Geist font, global CSS baseline, and application metadata before mounting the feature components explored in the next step.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router uses a file-system hierarchy where layout.tsx wraps every page.tsx beneath it. The root layout.tsx in src/app/ applies to the entire application, while nested layouts are scoped to their subtree. The 'use client' directive is only needed in files that use browser APIs or React state — server components are the default.

tour.title: Timetable Wizard UI
tour.description: TimetableApp.tsx is the heart of the frontend experience: a multi-step wizard component that guides users through schedule setup, teacher and subject entry, class assignments, constraint specification, AI-driven generation, and the final spreadsheet view. It is the most connected file on the client side, importing the API key store, the AI client, the shared types, application-wide constants, and utilities. This is where the user's timetable inputs become an API request.
tour.languageLesson: Large React wizard components are often split into 'steps' rendered conditionally by a state variable (e.g., currentStep). Each step is a separate sub-component or JSX block, keeping the root component as a state machine that orchestrates transitions rather than rendering everything inline.

tour.title: Domain Types & Constants
tour.description: types.ts and constants.ts establish the shared vocabulary used across the entire timetable feature. types.ts defines the TypeScript interfaces for request payloads, solver results, violation records, SSE progress events, and solver statistics — the contract between the UI, the API client, and the backend service. constants.ts supplies school day definitions, session configurations, default period counts, class/subject presets, teacher color palettes, and Tailwind CSS class strings shared by multiple components.
tour.languageLesson: Separating domain types from implementation is a common TypeScript pattern. Keeping all shared interfaces in a single types.ts file makes it easy to find every data structure, avoids circular imports between feature modules, and provides a single source of truth when the API contract evolves.

tour.title: API Key Management
tour.description: api-key-store.ts is a Zustand store that manages the AI API key lifecycle: storage in localStorage, quota polling, and model-policy data. ApiKeyScreen.tsx renders the key-entry screen that validates a submitted key against the backend before persisting it, while QuotaDisplay.tsx polls the store and shows live token-usage numbers. Together these three files implement the API key feature slice — new visitors are gated by this screen before they can generate their first timetable.
tour.languageLesson: Zustand is a minimal React state library that stores state outside the component tree. Unlike useState or useReducer, a Zustand store is a singleton module that any component can import and subscribe to without prop-drilling or a Context provider.

tour.title: Client-Side AI Interface
tour.description: client.ts is the thin HTTP client that TimetableApp.tsx calls to kick off timetable generation. It supports both a regular JSON response and a streaming Server-Sent Events (SSE) mode, forwarding each progress event to a callback so the wizard can display live generation status. This file is the boundary between the client-side wizard and the server-side agentic loop — everything above this file lives in the browser, everything below runs on the server.
tour.languageLesson: Server-Sent Events (SSE) use a long-lived HTTP connection where the server pushes newline-delimited 'data:' lines. The browser's EventSource API (or a manual ReadableStream reader in fetch) consumes them as they arrive. SSE is simpler than WebSockets for one-directional server-to-client streaming and works through standard HTTP/2.

tour.title: Generation API Route
tour.description: route.ts (generate-timetable) is the Next.js POST handler that receives a timetable generation request from the client. It either streams progress events back via SSE or returns a plain JSON result, delegating the heavy lifting immediately to the agentic loop service. This file is the most-depended-upon server file (fan-in: 4), referenced by the README, the TypeScript config, the package manifest, and the shell smoke-test script, making it the architectural pivot between the frontend and the backend service.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router route handlers export named async functions (GET, POST, etc.) from a route.ts file. Returning a Response with a ReadableStream and Content-Type: text/event-stream enables SSE from a standard route handler without a dedicated WebSocket server.

tour.title: Agentic Generation Loop
tour.description: service.ts implements the core runAgenticLoop function: the multi-round orchestrator that drives timetable generation to a valid result. Each iteration calls an LLM (Coder Agent) to produce CP-SAT Python solver code, executes it in a sandboxed Python subprocess, runs deterministic constraint validation against the result, and — if violations remain — calls a second LLM (Checker Agent) with the violation report to guide the next iteration. The loop retries until the schedule passes all constraints or exhausts its attempt budget.
tour.languageLesson: Agentic loops (also called 'scaffolded LLM loops') combine an LLM for reasoning with deterministic tools for execution and verification. The pattern is: generate → execute → validate → feed errors back → repeat. This is more reliable than a single LLM call because deterministic checks catch hallucinations that the model itself cannot self-correct.

tour.title: Prompt Builders & Agent Templates
tour.description: coder.ts and checker.ts supply the structured prompt builder functions used by the agentic loop. coder.ts builds the prompt for the Coder Agent, which must generate a Python function named solve_timetable that follows a strict CP-SAT contract. checker.ts builds the prompt for the Checker Agent, which evaluates solver output against the normalized problem and deterministic violation reports to decide whether to pass or request a retry. timetable-config.ts centralizes the numeric knobs (iteration limits, prompt truncation thresholds, LLM retry delays) shared by both agents.

tour.title: Problem Normalization & Constraints
tour.description: timetable-problem.ts transforms a raw UI request into a NormalizedSolverProblem — mapping teachers, classes, subjects, and slots into a canonical form the solver understands. It calls constraint-parser.ts, which uses a regex-based, deterministic Vietnamese-language parser to convert freeform constraint text (teacher availability windows, consecutive period limits, daily subject quotas) into structured ParsedConstraint objects. deterministic-checker.ts then uses those parsed constraints to verify any candidate solution before it is accepted.
tour.languageLesson: A deterministic checker alongside an LLM is a powerful reliability pattern. The regex-based constraint parser is 100% predictable — given the same input it always produces the same output — which means test coverage is tractable. The LLM is reserved for the creative task (generating solver code) where rules cannot be exhaustively enumerated.

tour.title: Python Solver Bridge
tour.description: sandbox.ts is the Node.js-to-Python bridge at the center of the execution layer (fan-in: 4). It spawns the Python timetable_solver subprocess, resolves the correct paths to the runner script and Python binary in both development and Electron-packaged environments, enforces a configurable timeout, and parses the JSON result written to stdout. generated-solver-artifacts.ts complements it by managing the AI-generated Python solver files on disk — writing each iteration's code to a per-request directory and reading the base template for the Coder Agent's context.
tour.languageLesson: Spawning a subprocess with child_process.spawn (Node.js) allows a TypeScript server to delegate CPU-intensive or language-specific work to an external process. Using stdin/stdout as the IPC channel (sending JSON in, reading JSON out) keeps the interface simple and language-agnostic.

tour.title: Python Solver Engine
tour.description: runner.py is the Python-side CLI entry point: it reads a JSON solve request from stdin, dynamically loads the AI-generated solver module via importlib, calls its solve_timetable entrypoint, and writes a JSON result to stdout. The actual scheduling logic lives in template_solver.py, which translates domain rules (teacher conflicts, consecutive limits, free-day preferences, block scheduling) into OR-Tools CP-SAT model callbacks before delegating to base_solver_template.py's solve_base_model to run the solver and assemble the structured result.
tour.languageLesson: OR-Tools CP-SAT (Constraint Programming — Satisfiability) is a combinatorial optimization solver. A timetable is modeled as binary decision variables (assign_teacher_class_day_period) with constraints (no teacher in two places at once, maximum consecutive periods). CP-SAT searches for a solution satisfying all hard constraints and optimizing soft ones.

tour.title: Desktop App Packaging
tour.description: electron/main.mjs is the Electron main process that turns the web application into a cross-platform desktop app. It spawns the bundled Next.js server and the compiled Python solver as subprocesses, then loads the app URL in a BrowserWindow. The release-windows.yml GitHub Actions workflow orchestrates the full packaging pipeline: it compiles the Python solver with PyInstaller into a standalone executable, builds the Next.js bundle, packages everything with Electron Builder, and publishes the artifacts as a tagged GitHub release.
tour.languageLesson: Electron apps bundle a Node.js runtime and a Chromium renderer in a single executable. The main process (main.mjs) runs in Node.js and controls windows and system resources; the renderer process runs in the browser context. Spawning subprocesses from the main process (Next.js server, Python solver) is the standard pattern for embedding external servers in an Electron app.

tour.title: CI Pipeline & Tests
tour.description: ci.yml defines the automated quality gate that runs on every push and pull request: it installs JS and Python dependencies, runs ESLint, builds the Next.js app, executes the pytest unit suite against the Python solver, and conditionally runs dataset-driven API integration tests against a live dev server. test_datasets.py and the benchmark scripts in scripts/ complete the testing picture by validating solver correctness across a range of real school scheduling datasets.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use 'on' triggers (push, pull_request, workflow_dispatch), jobs for parallel execution, and steps for sequential work within a job. Conditional steps (if: env.RUN_INTEGRATION == 'true') let you gate expensive tests behind environment variables, keeping the default CI fast while allowing full validation on demand.

tour.title: Project Configuration
tour.description: package.json anchors the entire project: it declares the npm scripts (dev, build, lint, electron:build, python:runner), all frontend and tooling dependencies, and the project version (3.0.10). next.config.ts configures the Next.js build (standalone output mode for Electron bundling), while tsconfig.json, tailwind.config.ts, and components.json complete the TypeScript, CSS, and shadcn/ui configuration. Understanding these files explains why the project builds the way it does and which tools are available in each environment.
tour.languageLesson: next.config.ts output: 'standalone' mode bundles the Next.js server and its minimal node_modules subset into a self-contained .next/standalone directory. This is required for Electron packaging and Docker deployments where you cannot run 'npm install' at runtime.
